Meta.title: LocalCoder — AI coding that never leaves your building
Meta.description: Turnkey on-prem AI coding assistant for Czech enterprises. Apple Silicon hardware + RAG pipeline over your own codebase. Your code stays yours.

Footer.tagline: AI coding that never leaves your building.
Footer.parentCompany: A product by IC SERVIS s.r.o.
Footer.trademark: Apple, Mac, Mac Mini and Mac Studio are trademarks of Apple Inc., registered in the U.S. and other countries.

Hero.eyebrow: On-prem AI coding, built for Czech enterprises
Hero.subtitle: A turnkey local LLM coding assistant that indexes your codebase and runs on Apple Silicon — so your source, secrets, and IP never leave your building.

Problem.title: Your code is your most valuable asset. Cloud AI treats it like training data.
Problem.subtitle: Three reasons Czech engineering leaders are pulling back from cloud coding assistants.
Problem.card1Body: Every prompt to Copilot or Cursor ships proprietary source and secrets to third-party infrastructure. Once it's sent, you don't control where it lands.
Problem.card2Body: EU AI Act obligations land August 2026. Regulated industries — banking, healthcare, defense — need provable data residency and audit trails.
Problem.card3Body: A 20-person team on Claude Max or Copilot Enterprise burns €2,000–5,000 a month. Local inference pays for itself in under a quarter.

HowItWorks.title: How it works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Four steps. One box. Zero code leaving your network.
HowItWorks.step1Body: AST-aware chunking with tree-sitter extracts semantic units from your entire codebase — functions, classes, docs — and embeds them locally.
HowItWorks.step2Body: Hybrid semantic + keyword search pulls only the most relevant chunks into context. 3.4× smaller payloads than naive grep.
HowItWorks.step3Body: A local coding model (Qwen, DeepSeek, or GLM) produces grounded, codebase-aware answers on your hardware — never ours.
HowItWorks.step4Body: Developers keep their IDE. OpenCode, Aider, Continue.dev, VS Code and JetBrains all talk to the same OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

Benchmarks.title: Real numbers from our reference pipeline.
Benchmarks.subtitle: Measured on a 16 GB M1 Pro with Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B across Express.js, Next.js and a private Swift codebase.

HardwareTeaser.title: One box per team. Five tiers to choose from.
HardwareTeaser.subtitle: Pick the hardware that matches your team size, workload, and budget. We ship it pre-configured, installed, and supported.

HonestPositioning.title: What we do — and what we don't.
HonestPositioning.subtitle: We'd rather undersell and overdeliver. Here's the honest picture.
HonestPositioning.do1: Codebase Q&A: "where is the auth middleware?"
HonestPositioning.do3: Code review on your own style and conventions
HonestPositioning.do4: Everything that benefits from deep codebase context
HonestPositioning.dontTitle: When to still reach for cloud
HonestPositioning.dont1: Frontier-level architectural design on greenfield code
HonestPositioning.dont2: Complex multi-file reasoning across unfamiliar domains
HonestPositioning.dont3: Cutting-edge research coding where Opus-tier matters
HonestPositioning.dont4: We route those to your cloud provider with one toggle

CtaBand.subtitle: Two-week paid pilot. We bring the hardware, install it, index your repos, and measure what it does for your team.

Product.title: How LocalCoder actually works
Product.subtitle: The architecture, the pipeline, and the developer experience.

architecture.title: Four moving parts, one box
architecture.body: The whole stack runs on a single Apple Silicon machine inside your network. Nothing phones home.

editor.name: Editor
editor.body: OpenCode, Aider, Continue.dev, VS Code or JetBrains. Any OpenAI-compatible client works.

gateway.name: API gateway
gateway.body: Auth, usage tracking, RAG context injection. Lightweight FastAPI service.

vector.name: Vector index
vector.body: Qdrant running locally with AST-aware chunks from your repos. Git-hook fresh.

model.name: Local model
model.body: Swap models without changing the stack. Runs entirely on your hardware.

rag.title: The part that makes local actually work.
rag.body: Naive code search dumps whole files into the prompt and hopes. Our pipeline parses your codebase with tree-sitter, extracts semantic chunks — functions, classes, docstrings — and retrieves only what's relevant. Smaller context, faster answers, grounded in your actual code.
rag.feature1Body: Tree-sitter parses 7 languages. Functions stay intact, context headers included, cross-references tracked.
rag.feature2Body: Semantic embeddings + keyword search. Conceptual questions and exact lookups both work.
rag.feature3Body: Git hook re-indexes only changed files. A typical commit refreshes in 2–5 seconds.

benchmark.title: Measured, not promised.
benchmark.body: Real queries against Express.js, Next.js and a private Swift codebase. Same model, same machine.

integrations.title: Developers keep their tools
integrations.body: We expose an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Anything that talks to OpenAI talks to LocalCoder — no new CLI to learn.

cta.title: Two-week paid pilot on your hardware.

Hardware.title: Hardware tiers
Hardware.subtitle: Silent Apple Silicon boxes, each with a proven model and team-size fit.

included.title: What's included
included.item1Body: Pre-configured Apple Silicon box, delivered to your office.
included.item2Body: On-site installation, network integration, and staff training.
included.item3Body: Unlimited repos, AST-aware chunking, git hooks for live updates.
included.item4Body: 12 months of priority support, model updates, and security patches.

TierSelector.title: How many developers?
TierSelector.body: Drag the slider to see which tier fits your team.

RoiCalculator.title: See how fast it pays for itself.
RoiCalculator.disclaimer: Assumes 15% annual hardware opex (power, support, model updates) — does not factor the value of keeping your code on-prem.

Security.title: Your code never leaves the building
Security.subtitle: Air-gapped by default. GDPR-aligned. EU AI Act ready.

pillars.title: How we think about your code.
pillars.body: No gimmicks. No cloud smuggling. Six plain-English claims we put on paper — and that your CISO can verify in a single afternoon.
pillars.p1Body: The box runs fully offline. Optional outbound is allow-listed to your model mirror and nothing else.
pillars.p2Body: Source code, embeddings, logs, and session history live on hardware you control. No replication, no telemetry.
pillars.p3Body: Per-developer API keys, usage logs, and export-ready audit trails for your SIEM. SSO via OIDC on request.
pillars.p4Body: As a processor, we never receive customer data. DPAs ready on request; Czech/EU residency by construction.
pillars.p5Body: Coding assistants classify as minimal-risk. We provide technical documentation and a transparent risk statement.
pillars.p6Body: We use OSS components with clear licensing — Qdrant, Ollama, tree-sitter, OpenCode. No black boxes.

threat.title: Honest threat model
threat.body: What LocalCoder protects against — and what stays your job. We'd rather say so up front than hand-wave past it.
threat.protects1: Proprietary code leaking to third-party AI providers
threat.protects2: Prompts and codebase context crossing network boundaries
threat.protects3: Vendor lock-in — the stack is open and swappable
threat.protects4: Shadow IT: developers pasting code into public LLMs
threat.protects5: Data residency violations for EU-regulated industries
threat.yours1: Physical security of the hardware inside your building
threat.yours2: Network segmentation between dev machines and the box
threat.yours3: User provisioning, SSO, and off-boarding workflows
threat.yours4: Patching the underlying macOS and your IDE plugins
threat.yours5: Classifying which repos are safe to index

cta.title: Need the CISO one-pager?
cta.body: Email us — we'll send the architecture diagram, data flow, and a DPA template ready for your legal team.

About.title: Built in Prague, for Czech engineering teams
About.subtitle: A product by IC SERVIS s.r.o.
About.p1: LocalCoder is built in Prague by engineers who watched their clients get nervous every time a developer opened a cloud coding assistant. Proprietary code, regulated data, customer records — all of it was being streamed to third-party infrastructure with no audit trail.
About.p2: The technology to keep that code on-prem finally caught up in 2026. Apple Silicon unified memory, MLX-accelerated inference, and open models like Qwen 2.5 Coder and DeepSeek V3 made local coding assistants actually useful — not just a demo.
About.p3: We productized the hard parts — the RAG pipeline, the indexing, the IDE integration, the 12 months of support — so a Czech engineering team can have a Cursor-like experience without a single byte of source code leaving the building.
About.basedInBody: On-site visits anywhere in the CZ, SK and DACH region.
About.parentBody: Established s.r.o. providing IT services since 1999.
About.talkBody: We're happy to come to your office with a live demo on the actual hardware you'd deploy.

Pilot.title: Request a pilot
Pilot.subtitle: Tell us a bit about your team. We'll come back within two business days.
Pilot.step1Body: 45-minute video call. We learn about your stack, team, and security constraints.
Pilot.step2Body: Pre-configured Mac Mini or Studio shipped with OpenCode, RAG pipeline, and your repos indexed.
Pilot.step3Body: Your devs use it daily. We instrument it and report usage, latency, and cost savings weekly.
